meaning: n.跳跃adj.跳跃的,跳跃而行的
examples: Heidi ran into their midst, for she loved to see them leaping and playing about.
In any case, in animals it consists of leaping, running, climbing, throwing, wrestling, and other movements, either along, with objects, or with other animals.
The children were brazenly stripping off and leaping into the sea.
He has a habit of leaping onto your shoulders if you turn away too fast.
Science is leaping forward, yet the use of this data in medical decision-making is lagging behind.
